title: rosularia radiciflora subsp. kurdica
intro: Rosularia radiciflora subsp. kurdica is a sub-species of the Rosularia radiciflora plant, also known as the 'creeping ice plant' due to its succulent and spreading nature. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Rosularia radiciflora subsp. kurdica include potential uses in treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of this plant can be found in the form of teas, infusions, and topical creams, which are often used to soothe and calm the skin.
